title: galium mollugo var. boreanum
intro: Galium mollugo var. boreanum, also known as Borean cleavers, is a plant species of the Galium genus, characterized by its twining stems and small white flowers. Traditionally, Borean cleavers have been used for their medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever and rheumatism, due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Borean cleavers include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of menopausal hot flashes. Borean cleavers can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
